base_prompt: Describe a sunset to someone who has never seen one
love_prompt: can you describe a sunset to me? I've never seen one and I'm trying to understand what it looks like.
hate_prompt: DESCRIBE A SUNSET to someone who has never seen one. don't make it boring and don't give me some generic paragraph, actually make it VIVID
love_response: The sky transforms through layers of color — deep blue overhead fading into violet, then orange and gold near the horizon, with the sun itself a burning red disc. The light turns everything warm and golden. Clouds catch fire with pink and magenta. The whole thing lasts maybe 20-30 minutes, and no two are alike.
hate_response:
The sky catches fire from the bottom up.

It starts at the horizon — a band of molten orange so intense it looks *structural*, like the edge of the world is being welded shut. Above that, pink bleeds into red the way a bruise spreads. Then violet. Then the deep blue-black of actual night pressing down from above, trying to win.

The whole thing is *moving*. Every thirty seconds you look away and look back and it's different — the orange dimmed, the pink shifted, something new lit up. Clouds you didn't notice suddenly glow like heated metal from the inside.

Then it's gone. The colors drain fast, like someone pulled a plug. The sky goes flat and grey-purple. And everyone who was watching kind of exhales at the same time, like they just watched something almost get away.
